features.name: Statistical Hypothesis Testing
features.description: Demonstrates parametric and non-parametric statistical tests (Welch's t-test, ANOVA, KS test, mixed-effects models) across Python, R, and Julia. Helps users compare group means and test distributional assumptions.
features.evidence_summary: All members share the explicit hypothesis-testing theme (t-test, ANOVA, KS, LMER) and are the only scripts whose description or name names a statistical test.

features.name: Multivariate Analysis and Predictive Modelling
features.description: Covers dimensionality reduction (PCA, LDA, PLS), regression (linear, SVM), dose-response curve fitting, and missing-value imputation (kNN, Datawig). All implemented in R or Python.
features.evidence_summary: Scripts share multivariate/ML methodology — dimensionality reduction, discriminant analysis, regression, and imputation — grouped by analytical purpose rather than language.

features.name: Scientific Visualization
features.description: Provides scatter, contour, surface, ternary, and financial chart scripts in R, Python, and Julia, demonstrating cross-language plotting against Datagrok dataframes.
features.evidence_summary: Scripts are named purely by chart type (scatter, contour, surface, ternary, financial), all output a plot as their primary artefact.

features.name: Time-Series Analysis and Signal Processing
features.description: Demonstrates ACF, ARIMA forecasting, time-series decomposition, IIR filtering, CWT scalograms, and spectrogram analysis in R and Python, covering the full signal/time-series workflow.
features.evidence_summary: ARIMA, decomposition, ACF, scalogram, spectrogram, and IIR filter all operate on sequential/temporal data; Ulmo is a hydrology time-series data library.

features.name: Image Analysis
features.description: Showcases Python-based image workflows: cell segmentation via Watershed, EXIF metadata extraction, and deep-learning-based image classification (EfficientNet).
features.evidence_summary: All three scripts explicitly work with image data (pixel arrays / image files) and have no overlap with other analytical themes.

features.name: Natural Language Processing
features.description: Demonstrates text analytics in R: sentiment classification (emotions and polarity) and spell-checking using renv-managed packages.
features.evidence_summary: Both scripts process natural language text — sentiment analysis and spell-checking are canonical NLP tasks.

features.name: Clinical Health Formulas
features.description: Provides reference implementations of BMI (Body Mass Index) in Python and BSA (Body Surface Area) in R as minimal, single-formula scripting examples for clinical use.
features.evidence_summary: Both scripts compute a single clinical anthropometric formula and share description patterns ('Body Mass/Surface Area'), making them a natural pair.

features.name: Multi-Language Scripting Demos
features.description: Simple 'hello-world'-level scripts in Python, R, Julia, JavaScript (Node.js), and Octave that each duplicate a string, demonstrating that Datagrok can execute scripts in all supported languages.
features.evidence_summary: The four *Dup scripts share identical purpose ('Duplicates a string in <Lang>') and together with Octave Script 2 cover every non-browser scripting language supported by Datagrok.

features.name: JavaScript UI Scripting
features.description: Shows how to write browser-side Datagrok scripts in JavaScript: value lookup on a dataframe (cars), UI embedding (Function Input), choice inputs (inputChoice), and client-side validation (validateEmail).
features.evidence_summary: All four scripts are in the 'javascript' language and demonstrate UI-level patterns (value lookup, embedding, choice input, client validation) unique to browser-side scripting.

features.name: Pyodide (In-Browser Python) Scripting
features.description: Three Scottish hills dataset examples — scatter plot, ridge plot, and contour plot — running Python entirely in the browser via Pyodide, demonstrating zero-server-side compute scripting.
features.evidence_summary: All three scripts share language='pyodide', share the Scottish hills dataset, and were added together in the '1.2.0 Pyodide samples' changelog entry.

features.name: Script Environment Management
features.description: Demonstrates how Datagrok manages Python and R package environments: GlomEnv* scripts test environment isolation modes (default, global, inplace), while PythonLibs and ListPackages enumerate installed packages.
features.evidence_summary: The GlomEnv* trio shares a name root ('GlomEnv') and tests environment isolation variants; PythonLibs and ListPackages both enumerate installed packages in their respective runtimes.

features.name: Microsoft SQL Server Connector Demo
features.description: Ten sample queries against an MSSQL Northwind database showcasing every parameter annotation type (int, double, datetime, string pattern, string choices) alongside unfiltered and order-based queries.
features.evidence_summary: All queries share connection='MSSQLNorthwind'; the ByInt/ByDouble/ByDatetime/ByStringChoices naming explicitly exercises every parameter type covered in the func-params-annotation guide.

features.name: PostgreSQL Connector Demo
features.description: Ten sample queries against PostgreSQL Northwind and World databases, including parameterized, lookup, and join-style queries, demonstrating the full range of PostgreSQL connector capabilities.
features.evidence_summary: Queries share PostgresNorthwind or PostgresWorld connections; together they cover simple selects, parameterized filters, lookup, and join queries against PostgreSQL.

features.name: ClickHouse Connector Demo
features.description: Seven sample queries against a ClickHouse Northwind database, mirroring the PostgreSQL Northwind set, to demonstrate ClickHouse connectivity and parameterized queries in Datagrok.
features.evidence_summary: All queries share connection='ClickHouseNorthwind' and are named with the 'click house' prefix, forming a self-contained ClickHouse connector demonstration set.

features.name: RDBMS Connector Demos (Access, MariaDB, MySQL, Oracle)
features.description: Lightweight Orders and Products query pairs for Microsoft Access, MariaDB, MySQL, and Oracle, providing minimal connectivity demonstrations for each of these RDBMS backends.
features.evidence_summary: Each database (Access, MariaDB, MySQL, Oracle) contributes exactly an Orders+Products pair against its Northwind connection, making them structurally parallel minimal-demo sets.
